37.reason: [If the first polynomial has only one term, the loop doesn't run, so 'i' remains 1, which is correct. However, if input ends immediately after first term with newline, this works. But if there's no newline (e.g., EOF), 'c' may not be '\n', leading to incorrect countx. Also, potential buffer overflow if more than 100 terms.]

55.reason: [When i == k-1, z[i+1] accesses out-of-bounds memory, leading to undefined behavior.]

64.reason: [This loop may also access z[j] beyond index k-1 if all remaining terms have same exponent, causing out-of-bounds read.]
